event: Arts Act 1951 establishes the Arts Council (An Chomhairle Ealaíon) as the statutory body for developing and promoting the arts in Ireland
Ireland joins the European Economic Community; Arts Council begins engaging with European cultural funding frameworks
Arts Plan published — first structured multi-year strategy for arts funding and development
Arts Act 2003 reconstitutes the Arts Council with an expanded mandate and updated governance framework
Budget cuts reduce Arts Council funding significantly during the financial crisis; grants to artists and organisations fall sharply
Culture 2025 government framework published; Arts Council aligned with national cultural strategy
Covid-19 emergency funding of over €25 million disbursed to support artists and arts organisations through the pandemic
Arts Council budget exceeds €130 million for the first time following significant government investment in culture
